notes: The cabin's personal-library slowstock. Boots from the Vol 7 Gallery section under 'Slowstock Library.' In-fiction publisher/date/hardware fields are authored below; the out-of-fiction Godot host reads act1_kwik_stop.json and act2_estuary.json in sequence.

shelf.label_subtitle: the summer at the kwik stop
shelf.shelf_condition: played · label sun-faded · cartridge sleeve intact
shelf.provenance_note: Third from the left on the second shelf. Behind the Estuary 2 case, which is a Betamax case with an Estuary 2 stick in it. Olaf kept them in Betamax cases because he liked the size.
shelf.cover_blurb: One clerk. One counter. One shift, and then something else. From Oneironautics Inc., the studio that made ESTUARY and ESTUARY 2 (and PIRATE SUMMER and MRS. WU'S GARDEN), a game about the kwik stop at 2:14 AM and the estuary that was, all along, underneath it. Recommended for players 12 and up. This game takes about twenty-five minutes to complete.
back_of_case: · A full year of Oregon weather.
· Three endings, one of them the one you wanted.
'They didn't know what to do with this game in 2003.  I don't know what to do with it now.  You should play it.'
— Ines Rocha, in the 2019 Slowstick Review Quarterly retrospective.
shelf.in_fiction_dev_note: Ines Rocha's junior-designer weekend build. The Kwik Stop half was the studio's failed Sam's Summer Shifts competitor from Q1 2003. The Estuary half was a template Ines had been building on her own time. She welded them together in three days over a Labor Day weekend. The team shipped it that October because they had nothing else.

cross_ref.callback_line: Tem, from the kitchen: 'you played Estuary 3.  I could tell.  It's the one where the store is the estuary.  I played it in eighth grade.  My grandfather bought it because Olaf told him to.'
